documentation.title: rectangular domain with point source in the center and ABC of 1st order
documentation.references: PhD thesis Maurerlehner 2023 TU Graz, TDEF paper in Acta Acustica Maurerlehner et al. 2025
documentation.description: 3D model of a semi-infinite duct for 1D plane-wave propagation in an inhomogeneous duct, consisting of three differenct fluids (air, equivalent fluid 1, equivalent fluid 2). The air is non-dissipative, whereas in the in the two different equivalent fluids, the TDEF formulation is applied. A pressure pulse propagates through the three domains, which is damped within the dissipative equivalent fluid domains and partially reflected at the interfaces due to the changing characteristic impedance. At the right boundary of the duct, the narrow-band non-reflecting boundary boundary condition (NRBC) is applied to avoid reflections. The left boundary (excitation BC) is reflective. Note that the target frequency for the NRBC differs from the source frequency because of the dispersive nature of the material, causing a shift in frequency of the pulse until it reaches the non-reflecting boundary. The rational function approximation of the equivalent fluid, required for the TDEF formulation is provided in the .mat file.
